branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Park & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Nationals pride

columnists.name: Grant Whitestone
columnists.backstory: A Capitol Hill reporter who covered the Expos-to-Nationals transition and still has his press pass from Game 7 in 2019.
columnists.voice_sample: Wood homered, Gray went six clean, and Washington beat the Phillies 4-2 at Nationals Park. Record evens at 8-8.

columnists.name: Wendy Okonkwo
columnists.backstory: A Petworth middle-school math teacher who was at Game 7 and keeps a vial of infield dirt in her desk drawer.
columnists.voice_sample: Elly Wood's line drives have a physics of their own. Rebuilds end in October parades, and this one's already got the smell.

columnists.name: Murray Feldschneider
columnists.backstory: A Georgetown retiree who refused to forgive the Lerner family for letting Harper walk and has written roughly a thousand words about it.
columnists.voice_sample: The Lerners sold a World Series and bought a minor-league reunion tour. This team has more gift-shop hats than wins in May.
